title: myosotis sparsiflora subsp. propinqua
intro: Myosotis sparsiflora subsp. propinqua, also known as Propinqua Forget-me-not, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Propinqua Forget-me-not include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Propinqua Forget-me-not typically involve infusing the leaves in hot water or making a decoction, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or topical creams for medicinal purposes.
